comment: timeframe — the reference bar clock (SPEC_REGISTERS.md §2). A pure-combinational rollover detector: when (TAI - BAR_START) >= PERIOD, BAR_SEQ += 1, BAR_CLOSED pulses 1 for one tick, BAR_START re-anchors to TAI. A GUIDE not a master: each bar module carries its own period multiple and closes its own bars by watching BAR_SEQ. Module barrier: TAI and PERIOD are presented on this device's own input lanes.

config_nodes.name: TF_POWER
config_nodes.comment: power bit: the rollover detect runs while bit0 = 1 (set once)

config_nodes.name: TF_RUN_UNTIL
config_nodes.comment: configured self-run tick budget (set once by starter)

config_nodes.name: TF_TAI_IN
config_nodes.comment: sampled TIME_TAI_NS_REG value presented on this device's input lane (module-barrier: not a raw read of the time source's registers)

config_nodes.name: TF_PERIOD_NS
config_nodes.comment: bar period in ns (= REG_TF_CONFIG / TF_PERIOD_NS_REG); operator-written once before the first tick

dff_nodes.name: TF_TICKS
dff_nodes.comment: self-run tick counter (run-loop bookkeeping; bounds the self-run)

dff_nodes.name: TF_BAR_SEQ
dff_nodes.comment: bar sequence; += 1 when elapsed >= period (= REG_TF_COUNTER rollover)

dff_nodes.name: TF_BAR_CLOSED
dff_nodes.comment: 0->1 one-tick pulse at a bar boundary (= TIMEFRAME_DIRTY_BIT)

dff_nodes.name: TF_BAR_START
dff_nodes.comment: TAI at the current bar open (= TIMEFRAME_CURRENT_CANDLE_OPEN)
